heroHeadline: Iran Rejects Ceasefire; Trump Threatens 'Power Plant Day' as April 6 Deadline Expires — War Day 36; March Jobs +178K Beats Estimates; 100% Pharma Tariff Signed
heroSubtitle: Trump's April 6 Strait of Hormuz deadline expired without agreement as Iran rejected the Pakistan/Egypt/Turkey 45-day ceasefire proposal and refused to reopen Hormuz without preconditions. Trump threatened 'Power Plant Day and Bridge Day' strikes, with oil surging back to ~$108–110/barrel. Meanwhile, the March 2026 jobs report crushed estimates (+178,000 vs. ~59,000 expected), unemployment fell to 4.3%, and Trump signed a 100% pharmaceutical import tariff EO and a college sports executive order. The Iran war entered its 36th day with no end in sight; Penn Wharton estimated total cost at $47 billion. Trump's approval remains below 40%.
footerNote: Data sourced from White House, Federal Register, executive orders, court records, Al Jazeera, CNN, Reuters, AP, SCOTUSblog, NPR, BLS, Peterson Institute for International Economics, Tax Foundation, CFR, EIA, Treasury, Goldman Sachs, Federal Reserve, OECD, Cato Institute, Penn Wharton Budget Model, Bloomberg, CNBC.
